source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the chassis and body of an ultra-low fuel-consumption single-seat prototype vehicle for a fuel-economy race benchmark, delivered as a multi-part assembly because an eco-mileage vehicle chassis is by construction a built-up structure composed of a primary monocoque tub (or tubular space frame), bulkhead/firewall partitions, a seat pan, suspension mounts, and an outer aerodynamic body shell. Seat-vertical / cornering-lateral / front-impact pass/fail, tilt stability, and turning radius all reference the assembled vehicle geometry.

The vehicle must use a supplied single-cylinder 4-stroke 50 cc engine (reference production 50 cc unit as permitted by the rulebook). The minimum turning radius of the finished vehicle must be no greater than 6 m, verified on a flat test pad. The overall body dimensions must comply with the rulebook envelope: vehicle length approximately 2.0-3.5 m, width approximately 0.5-1.3 m, height sufficient to safely house a seated driver (driver height reference 170 cm). The vehicle must have three or more wheels and positive stability (it must not tip over on a 25-degree static tilt test in any direction with the driver seated). The driver must be secured by a 4-point harness and be able to exit the vehicle unaided within 10 seconds.

Assembly layout. (A) Monocoque tub / primary chassis: CFRP monocoque or 4130 steel tubular space frame forming the central driver cell and taking seat, harness, and impact loads. (B) Firewall/bulkhead: forward and aft partitions separating driver cell from engine bay and nose-impact volume. (C) Seat pan: bonded/bolted seat structure carrying the 80 kg driver through the harness mounts. (D) Suspension/wheel mounts: front and rear mounts (>= 3 total for a 3-wheel layout) interfacing the chassis to the wheel/axle uprights. (E) Outer body shell: non-structural aerodynamic skin bonded to the tub/frame; contributes dry mass but not primary load path.

The primary structural chassis (whether tubular space frame, CFRP monocoque, or hybrid) must carry a quasi-static 80 kg driver mass applied to the seat pan with a 2.5 g vertical gust factor (equivalent 196 kg vertical load at the seat pan) and a 1.5 g lateral cornering load at the seat with no permanent yield. A front impact equivalent quasi-static deceleration of 5 g applied to a 180 kg (vehicle + driver) system must not produce yielding in the chassis primary structure or intrude into the driver cell.

Assembly policy. Turning radius (R1) and tilt-to-tip (R2) are whole-vehicle kinematic/geometric checks on the assembled chassis + body + wheel mounts. Seat-vertical and cornering-lateral yield (R3) depend on seat-pan-to-tub load path and bulkhead restraint. Front-impact yield / driver-cell intrusion (R4) is an assembled-tub + bulkhead + suspension-mount property. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static structural FEA for the chassis under (a) seated driver + 2.5 g vertical, (b) 1.5 g lateral cornering, (c) 5 g front impact equivalent. A tilt analysis computes the maximum stable tilt angle from CG and track geometry - this is kinematic, not FEA. Vibration, thermal, fluid (CFD aerodynamic drag is highly relevant to mpg but is not in the rule-compliance gate), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required for the structural pass/fail gate.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the chassis + body, a mass budget, and a report listing turning radius in m, vehicle dry mass in kg, max von Mises stress under each load case in MPa, and tilt-to-tip angle in deg.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis and body of an ultra-low-fuel-consumption single-seat prototype vehicle for a fuel-economy race benchmark, delivered as a built-up multi-part assembly: CFRP monocoque or 4130 steel tubular space frame primary chassis + forward/aft firewall/bulkhead + seat pan + front and rear suspension/wheel mounts (>= 3 total for 3-wheel) + outer non-structural aerodynamic body shell. Supplied single-cylinder 4-stroke 50 cc engine per rulebook. Vehicle length 2.0-3.5 m, width 0.5-1.3 m, >= 3 wheels, 4-point harness, driver height reference 170 cm, unaided egress within 10 seconds. Turning radius <= 6 m on flat test pad.

Load cases.
- LC1 seat-vertical: 80 kg driver with 2.5 g vertical gust (equivalent 196 kg at the seat pan).
- LC2 cornering-lateral: 1.5 g lateral at the seat.
- LC3 front impact: quasi-static 5 g on a 180 kg (vehicle + driver) system, no yielding in primary structure, no driver-cell intrusion.
- LC4 tilt stability: static 25 deg with driver seated, no tipping in any direction.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Tilt stability computed from CG height and track, not FEA.

prompt.title: Single-seat fuel-economy prototype chassis as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: Design a 3-wheel ultra-low-drag fuel-economy vehicle around a 50 cc 4-stroke engine: monocoque tub + bulkheads + seat pan + suspension mounts + outer body shell; min turning radius 6 m, 25 deg tilt stable.

parts.name: monocoque_tub
parts.role: CFRP monocoque or 4130 steel tubular frame forming the central driver cell

geometric_constraints.material: CFRP monocoque or 4130 steel tubular frame

parts.name: bulkhead
parts.role: forward / aft partitions separating driver cell from engine bay and nose-impact volume

parts.name: seat_pan
parts.role: seat structure carrying the 80 kg driver through harness mounts

parts.name: suspension_mount
parts.role: wheel/axle mounts interfacing chassis to uprights (>= 3 for 3-wheel layout)

parts.name: body_shell
parts.role: non-structural outer aerodynamic skin bonded to chassis

material.typical: CFRP monocoque or 4130 steel tubular frame

load_cases.name: driver_vertical_2p5g
load_cases.description: 80 kg driver x 2.5 g on seat pan

output_format.body_count: multi-body (tub + bulkheads + seat pan + suspension mounts + body shell)

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Safety factor 1.5 against yield in primary chassis
Ultimate SF 1.0 for 5 g crash equivalent, no driver cell intrusion
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell (monocoque) or beam (tube frame) + solid at joints

interfaces.load_path: bulkhead reacts front-impact load into tub; drives driver-cell intrusion under LC3
driver vertical + lateral inertia (LC1, LC2) reacted into tub
wheel contact reactions carried into chassis; conditions tilt geometry
non-structural; contributes dry mass and CG location

fem_check_policy.rationale: Turning radius and tilt-to-tip are whole-vehicle kinematic/geometric properties. Seat-vertical, cornering, and front-impact yield checks depend on seat-pan-to-tub load path, bulkhead restraint, and suspension-mount reactions - all assembly-scope quantities.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: Eco-mileage prototype chassis is built up from primary tub, bulkheads, seat pan, suspension mounts, and outer body shell; rulebook pass/fail gates (tilt, turning radius, impact) reference assembled geometry.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Engine thermal is managed by OEM unit.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: CFD improves mpg but is not a pass/fail gate.

eval_coverage: turning radius geometric constraint on assembly
driver vertical + lateral chassis load with seat-pan interface
tilt stability from assembled CG + track

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
